anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Theistic surrender and grace traditions, for example Christian apophatic prayer or Islamic tawakkul, may treat relation to God as irreducible, not as de-emphasis of agency or boundary-making.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence from candidate-discovery range to novel-synthesis range.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If the practitioner says the core invariant is not an altered interface but a real Other who initiates and judges the transformation, the model becomes a reduction rather than an interpretation.

claim_units: Independent traditions can converge without sharing a metaphysical object.
The shared structure is the human interface of attention, identity, salience, agency, and boundaries.
Witness-consciousness, emptiness, nondual awareness, wu wei, and surrender may report alterations of that interface.
The model preserves comparative value while blocking naive perennialism.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Immersive systems that alter these variables should generate report patterns resembling mild contemplative shifts, without requiring spiritual metaphysics.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Interface invariants become attention routing, agency attribution, salience marking, avatar identity, and boundary control.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Altering interface variables changes task performance but does not produce predictable shifts in self, agency, salience, or boundary reports.

exact_claim: Cross-tradition convergence may come from recurring human interface invariants such as attention, identity, salience, agency, and boundary-making, rather than from discovery of the same hidden metaphysical substance.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Blind coders should reliably tag these interface variables in practitioner reports better than chance, while also preserving doctrinal differences.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Cross-tradition reports of witness, emptiness, surrender, and wu wei should cluster by shifts in attention, agency, salience, identity, and boundary experience even when doctrinal vocabulary differs.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Qualitative coding of meditation, prayer, and contemplative-inquiry interviews across Advaita, Theravada, Daoist, Sufi, and Christian sources.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Reports cluster mainly by tradition-specific doctrine, devotional object, or teacher vocabulary, with weak cross-tradition interface structure.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Practice instructions with similar attention and agency moves should produce similar near-term phenomenological reports even when ontology differs.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Low-metaphysical comparison using interface variables should predict practice similarities better than metaphysical labels such as Self, emptiness, God, or nature.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Matched practitioner interviews before and after narrowly specified practices.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Metaphysical training context predicts reports more strongly than interface variables do.

literature_search_queries: interface invariants attention identity salience agency boundary-making spiritual traditions convergence
Thomas Metzinger minimal phenomenal experience pure awareness witness consciousness
Steven Katz Language Epistemology and Mysticism constructivism pure experience
Varela Thompson Rosch embodied mind Buddhism enactive self no self
Robert Forman pure consciousness event comparative mysticism

near_neighbors.difference: The Lumenary model substitutes a cognitive-interface invariant list for the older universal core or constructivist framing.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The core alternative to metaphysical sameness is already central in comparative mysticism.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close at the level of the perennialist versus constructivist problem. The entry covers Stace's universal introvertive mysticism, Forman's pure consciousness event, and Katz's claim that conceptual schemes shape mystical experience.

near_neighbors.difference: The paper is a phenomenological questionnaire and does not make a full cross-tradition convergence theory for spiritual motifs such as wu wei and surrender.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the invariant list. Some originality remains in using it as a comparative-audit frame.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. The MPE-92M factors include wakeful presence, emptiness and non-egoic self-awareness, mental agency, and witness consciousness, which overlap strongly with the proposed interface variables.
near_neighbors.source: Gamma and Metzinger, The Minimal Phenomenal Experience questionnaire, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8279394/

near_neighbors.difference: The Interface Invariant Model names a smaller audit-ready set of invariants and compares several traditions rather than building one enactive cognitive-science program.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction. The world-disclosure move is old, but the Lumenary operationalization may be useful.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Enactivism already reframes cognition as world-disclosure through embodied action and was built in dialogue with Buddhist no-self and mindfulness.
near_neighbors.source: Varela, Thompson, and Rosch, The Embodied Mind, MIT Press overview, https://mitpress.mit.edu/9780262529365/the-embodied-mind/

near_neighbors.difference: Metzinger is not primarily making a comparative religion publication rule for convergence claims.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the consciousness and self-model components.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Metzinger explicitly studies pure awareness, self-luminosity, sakshin, turiya, rigpa, and minimal phenomenal selfhood as a route to modeling consciousness.

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish this as a metaphysical claim.
Build a five-variable coding sheet and test it on narrow primary-text pairs before using broad tradition names.
Add a devotional anomaly set: grace, surrender, bhakti, tawakkul, and Christian apophasis.
Separate interface change, doctrinal interpretation, and claimed ontological referent in all future records.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.76 to about 0.52. The idea is a useful synthesis of comparative mysticism, constructivism, enactivism, self-model theory, and minimal phenomenal experience research, not a discovered structure yet.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners and scholars say this is just constructivism, enactivism, MPE research, or perennialism rewritten in cognitive-interface language.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: They say the invariant list changes how they compare practices, predicts misunderstandings, or helps distinguish phenomenological overlap from metaphysical agreement.
questions: Do you already compare traditions by shifts in attention, agency, salience, identity, and boundaries?
Does this frame reveal anything about practice that your tradition's own vocabulary does not already capture?
Where would this model reduce or distort the meaning of your practice?

primary_pair: SN 22.59 Anattalakkhana Sutta, Thanissaro Bhikkhu translation, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/SN/SN22_59.html
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model can silently translate ontological, devotional, ritual, and soteriological claims into cognitive-interface language. It may explain reports, but it does not by itself interpret traditions on their own terms.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Search found strong near-neighbors in constructivist mysticism, Stace and Forman on pure consciousness, enactivism, Metzinger's minimal phenomenal experience work, self-model theory, and neurophenomenology. No source found the exact five-part interface list as a comparative-spirituality rubric, but the structural move is heavily precedented.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: The pair supports only part of the model. Brihadaranyaka 3.7.23 repeatedly places an inner ruler within earth, beings, organs, mind, and knowledge, then identifies this as Self and immortal. SN 22.59 examines form, feeling, perception, fabrications, and consciousness, then rejects each as mine, self, or what I am because each is inconstant and not masterable. Both disturb ordinary identity and boundary-making, but their final authority structure diverges sharply: one licenses an unobjectifiable Self, the other de-authorizes ownership of all aggregates including consciousness.

title: The Interface Invariant Model
unlike_statement: Unlike Stace, Forman, Katz, Varela, Thompson, Rosch, or Metzinger, this idea proposes a Lumenary comparison unit in which apparent spiritual convergence is scored by recurring changes to attention, identity, salience, agency, and boundary-making, without inferring one shared metaphysical object.
